input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the full setup:

**AppShell.xaml**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell x:Class="MyApp.AppShell"
       xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
       xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
       xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyApp.Views"
       FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
       Title="MyApp">

    <!-- Dashboard – single page, own flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent Title="Dashboard"
                      ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products – two tabs under one flyout item -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent Title="Active"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}" />
            <ShellContent Title="Archived"
                          ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings – separate flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent Title="Settings"
                      ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs** — register the detail route here:

```csharp
namespace MyApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register routes for pages you navigate to programmatically
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Views.ProductDetailsPage),
                              typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Navigating to the detail page** (e.g. from `ActiveProductsPage`):

```csharp
// pass the product id as a query parameter
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

**Receiving the parameter** in `ProductDetailsPage`:

```csharp
[QueryProperty(nameof(ProductId), "id")]
public partial class ProductDetailsPage : ContentPage
{
    public string ProductId { get; set; }
}
```

### Key points

| Element | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `FlyoutItem` | Top-level entry in the flyout menu |
| `Tab` wrapping two `ShellContent`s | Creates bottom tabs (Active / Archived) inside one flyout item |
| `ContentTemplate` + `DataTemplate` | Lazy-loads pages on first visit |
| `Routing.RegisterRoute` | Enables `GoToAsync` navigation to pages not in the visual hierarchy |

`ProductDetailsPage` is registered as a *route-only* page — it doesn't appear in the flyout but is pushed onto the navigation stack when you call `GoToAsync`.

1. A flyout menu with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. A Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage

This is a straightforward question - I can answer directly without needing to explore any codebase.
